title: Online Press Kit
description: An online press kit for media that provides an overview of RAND. High-resolution photos of the buildings and experts are available on request.
text:
What is RAND?
Some people call us a think tank but research organization is a more fitting descriptor given our nonprofit status and non-ideological approach to helping improve policy and decisionmaking through research and analysis. RAND focuses on the issues that matter most, such as health, education, national security, international affairs, law and business, the environment, and more. For more detail check out our about page, our history, our annual report, or Frequently Asked Questions.
How can I see your research?
RAND reports are available for free at rand.org and you are welcome to cite this research in news articles. You can search by title, author, research category, or document number. We also provide free hard copies to journalists. Just visit our press room. We would also encourage you to follow us on LinkedIn, Facebook, X, and Instagram, and subscribe to our various RSS feeds.
How can I speak with your researchers?
Visit our press room and we'll help arrange an interview. We would also encourage you to peruse our online RAND Policy Experts Guide, replete with researcher bios.
For TV interviews:
We can accommodate camera crews at RAND offices. Our researchers also often travel to nearby TV studios and can conduct interviews remotely.
For radio interviews:
We have ISDN facilities at the Santa Monica and Washington/Arlington, VA offices.
What about photos?
To request RAND photos, please fill out the photo request form.
What about video?
We have our own multimedia site with videos you can link to, as well as a YouTube channel, which allows you to embed our videos in your site. Small portions of an independent documentary about RAND produced in 2008 are available for B-roll purposes; visit our press room for more info.
What about audio?
We post audio files of select news events and have podcast series that you can subscribe to: Policy Minded and Events @ RAND.
I'm a student journalist — may I speak with your experts?
Visit our press room and we'll try, but please bear in mind our researchers are not always able to accommodate student requests due to time commitments.
Who runs RAND?
Our management team is led by president and CEO Jason Matheny. The Chairperson of our Board of Trustees is Michael E. Leiter.
Who does RAND work for?
RAND has a global clientele that includes government agencies, foundations, other nonprofit organizations, and private-sector firms. Additionally, philanthropic contributions, combined with earnings from RAND's endowment, support research on issues crucial to the policy debate but that reach beyond the boundaries of traditional client funding.
Who does the research?
Our project teams are assembled from RAND's research professionals from academia, government, and industry whose collective expertise ranges from economics and behavioral science to medicine and engineering. RAND researchers combine theory with real-world experience.
Where are your offices?
RAND is a global organization with offices in North America, Europe, and Australia. RAND's U.S. locations include Santa Monica, California, the home of its headquarters campus and the RAND School of Public Policy; Arlington, Virginia; Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania; and Boston, Massachusetts. RAND also has U.S.-based researchers living and working in many other states and the District of Columbia. RAND Europe has offices in Cambridge, United Kingdom; Brussels, Belgium; and The Hague, The Netherlands. RAND Australia has its office in Canberra.
No, sorry. RAND only publishes work by RAND researchers. We do not accept outside submissions or guest posts.
What if I have other questions?
